name: kattegat-all-angles-demo
description: Round-2 all-angles demo, Danish waters (Kattegat / Great Belt), same region as the recorded aisdk replay so Act 1 and Act 2 share one map. Three vessels exercise every detection path the engine has. GRETHE HANSEN (Danish, MMSI 219000111) is the protected-zone offender and the CRITICAL money shot: it crosses into the Great Belt Natura 2000 MPA (ZONE_VIOLATION, inline), works a slow weaving trawl track inside it (TRAWLING, gated on illegal-here; weather layer tags it calm-water HIGH confidence when online), then dashes out and stops transmitting just north of the park (DARK_EVENT with a dead-reckoning cone and an intercept solution for HDMS Rota / HDMS Diana). Its risk score is 90 (zone) +20 (fishing) +15 (dark) capped to 100, so it labels CRITICAL and fires ILLEGAL_FISHING_SUSPECTED. NORTH STAR (foreign / NL, MMSI 244000222) is the deception-and-absence climber near the Anholt wind farm: it teleports once (SPOOF_TELEPORT, inline) then goes dark five times in a row near the closed wind farm without ever entering it, so its score has no 90-point zone term and climbs honestly LOW -> ELEVATED -> HIGH (spoof 15 + stacked dark 15/10/10/10/10), firing ILLEGAL_FISHING_SUSPECTED on the HIGH crossing with each dark carrying its own cone and intercepts. DFDS PEARL (Danish ferry, MMSI 219000333) is the control: an honest transit that stays clear of both MPAs and keeps transmitting, so it never alerts and never scores. That is the precision story on screen: clean traffic stays quiet. 'speed' compresses playback 60x; frame t_ms are event times so implied speeds are honest and only the intended teleport trips spoof. Dark reveal is wall-time (6x the reporting interval): the two fast runners vanish at 25 kn, ~12s of real silence, well inside the demo window, each heading toward its stationed patrol so at least one intercept solution is feasible.
